title: Movement Rule
description: Defines whether a goods movement between two areas is functionally permitted. Independent of physical reachability (see lanes.yaml).

applies_to_policy.description: Must match the movement_policy of the affected storage_type.

execution.description: Who carries out this leg. 'automated' = executed by the automation, i.e. both endpoints sit under the same downstream controller (storage_type.controller / reporting_point.controller), so the controller's equipment performs it. 'manual' = a human/operator leg, no equipment involved. Optional, and redundant with the endpoints' controllers by design - tools/validate.py flags an execution value that contradicts what the endpoints imply. The finer automation mode (controller_autonomous vs wms_controlled) lives on equipment.mode, not here.

conversion_of_load_unit_required.description: True when the load unit type changes across this leg (e.g. pallet -> piece, or a bin's contents repacked into a different carrier). When set, allowed_load_unit_types names the SOURCE type present at 'from', not the resulting type at 'to' - there is no separate field for the resulting type.

trigger.description: References an id in elements/process_types.yaml, e.g. 'putaway_task', 'replenishment_task', 'pick_task'.

idOrIds.description: A single id, or a list of ids that all play the identical role in this rule (e.g. several ports fed by the same movement). Use a list purely to avoid repeating an otherwise-identical rule per id - if the ids ever need different allowed_load_unit_types, trigger, execution, etc., split them back into separate rules instead.
